self_report.reflection: Setting up Area(A) = Area(B) and combining the integrals leads to c·x₂ − x₂² + (3/4)x₂⁴ = 0, i.e. c = x₂ − (3/4)x₂³. Together with c = 2x₂ − 3x₂³ (the on-curve condition at x₂), this forces x₂² = 4/9, so x₂ = 2/3 and c = 4/3 − 8/9 = 4/9. The value lies below the curve's maximum 4√2/9 ≈ 0.628, so the geometry is valid.
self_report.notes: Direct algebraic derivation reproduces c = 4/9 exactly, matching the standard Putnam 1993 A1 answer.
